# RepoBrain — Pitch Deck Outline (12 slides)

*Generated by launch-package skill | 2026-04-14*
*Format: YC-style, 2:15 presentation, 10–12 slides*

---

## Slide 1 — Title

**Title:** RepoBrain

**Punchline:**
Navigate any codebase — from first question to confident answer.

**Speaker note:**
RepoBrain is a codebase intelligence layer that answers questions about code by retrieving real evidence from the repo — not guessing from training data. Every answer cites a real file:line.

---

## Slide 2 — Problem

**Headline:** Engineers spend 30% of their time navigating code they didn't write.

**Bullets:**
- Senior engineers at 10–200-person teams spend 2–3 days per sprint reading code before they can change it safely
- "Where does X happen?" is the most common question on any engineering team — it has no fast answer today
- Code review, onboarding, and incident response all require understanding code you don't know — under pressure
- Existing tools (Copilot Chat, grep, documentation) don't know your repo — they hallucinate or miss the context entirely

**Speaker note:**
The core problem isn't that codebases are too complex — it's that understanding is completely manual. Every engineer starts from zero every time they touch an unfamiliar part of the codebase. We built RepoBrain to fix that.

---

## Slide 3 — Solution

**Headline:** RepoBrain: a third-person intelligence layer for your codebase.

**Bullets:**
- Ask any question about your code in natural language — get sourced answers with real file:line citations
- Three retrieval strategies run in parallel: semantic (intent), lexical (keywords), structural (symbol relations)
- The AI is instructed to only cite what retrieval returned — citation enforcement at the prompt level
- 15 features: conversations, search, blast radius, patterns, what-if, architecture diagrams, specialist agents, and more

**Demo visual descriptor:**
Screen split: left = "Where does request auth happen?" typed in the chat bar; right = answer with three citations highlighted — auth.ts:47, middleware.ts:23, session.ts:89. One click opens the line in the code viewer.

**Speaker note:**
The demo is the point. Other tools answer from training data — which means they can invent file paths. RepoBrain answers from your repo — which means every answer is verifiable in one click.

---

## Slide 4 — Demo

**Headline:** RepoBrain in 3 steps

**3-step flow:**

1. **Connect:** Authorize GitHub, select a repo. RepoBrain clones and indexes it — symbol extraction, embedding generation, relation mapping — in under 10 minutes.
2. **Ask:** Type a question in natural language. Three retrieval strategies find the relevant context in parallel and assemble it for the LLM.
3. **Verify:** The answer arrives with cited file:line ranges. Click any citation to open the exact line. Every claim checkable in one click.

**Screen/recording descriptor:**
30-second screen recording: brief form opens (5s) → indexing progress bar completes (5s) → question typed (5s) → answer with inline citations appears (10s) → click on citation, code viewer opens to exact line (5s).

**Speaker note:**
The "click the citation" moment is the demo. That's when engineers stop being skeptical — they see the AI pointing at real code they recognize. It's not magic; it's retrieval done right.

---

## Slide 5 — Market Size

**Headline:** $25B+ developer tools market. RepoBrain starts in the AI code intelligence layer.

**Bullets:**
- **TAM:** $25B — Global developer tools and DevEx software market (IDC 2025)
- **SAM:** $4.2B — AI-assisted code understanding tools for teams of 10–500 (28M professional developers, 15% with team-size/usage fit, $10/mo average)
- **SOM:** $42M ARR — Realistic 3-year capture: 35,000 teams at $29/mo Pro average, 10% churn
- Developer tools with strong PLG motions reach $100M ARR with <$500K total ad spend (Linear: $35K lifetime, Vercel: organic)

**Speaker note:**
The SAM is the number that matters. AI code tools are the fastest-growing segment of developer tooling right now — GitHub Copilot alone has 1.8M paid seats, but it's a code completion tool. RepoBrain is the first tool in this space whose only job is codebase comprehension. That's an uncrowded position.

## Slide 7 — Business Model

**Headline:** Freemium SaaS. $29/mo Pro. Estimated 82% gross margin.

**Bullets:**
- **Free tier:** 1 repo, 4 core features (Conversations, Search, Blast Radius, Architecture Diagrams) — trust hook, no credit card
- **Pro ($29/mo):** All 15 features, 10 repos, 3 team members, specialist agents (Code Review, Security Audit, ADR Generator)
- **Team ($99/mo):** Unlimited repos, 25 team members, Cross-Repo Intelligence, priority support
- **Gross margin:** ~82% (infrastructure: Postgres, Redis, embedding API calls — no sales team, no COGS)
- **Expansion motion:** Solo engineer → team → org-wide workspace as reliability is proven

**Speaker note:**
The free tier is the onboarding funnel. An engineer who connects one repo and gets a good answer in 10 minutes will pay $29/mo. The conversion vector is personal experience, not a demo call. We're modeling 8% free-to-Pro conversion based on comparable PLG developer tools.

---

## Slide 8 — Competition

**Headline:** RepoBrain is the first tool that grounds every answer in your actual codebase.

**Competition quadrant:**

```
Y-axis (top): High answer grounding (cites your actual code)
              │
RepoBrain ●  │
              │
              │              ● Sourcegraph (search, some AI)
              │
              │   ● GitHub Copilot Chat    ● ChatGPT + code
              │
Y-axis (low) ─┼─────────────────────────────────────────────
           (low X)  Retrieval depth   (high X)
             (keywords only)     (semantic + lexical + structural)
```

**Competitors:**
- **GitHub Copilot Chat** — In-editor chat, no repo grounding, frequent hallucinations on large codebases
- **Sourcegraph** — Strong code search, limited AI reasoning, primarily enterprise, complex self-hosted setup
- **ChatGPT / Claude with code** — General-purpose reasoning, no repo grounding, can't cite real line numbers
- **Grep/ripgrep** — Perfect precision, zero intelligence — you must already know what you're looking for

**Speaker note:**
The axes are the unique mechanism: retrieval depth (can it find semantically similar code, not just keyword matches?) and answer grounding (does it cite your actual code or hallucinate?). We're the only tool that's high on both. Sourcegraph is close on retrieval but weak on AI reasoning. Copilot Chat is the inverse.

## Slide 10 — Financials

**Headline:** $1M ARR by Month 18. Product-led, no outbound required.

**18-month projection:**

| Month | Revenue | Burn | Milestone |
|-------|---------|------|-----------|
| M3 | $12K MRR | $25K/mo | Public launch, 50+ Pro teams |
| M6 | $35K MRR | $30K/mo | Team tier launch, first $400K ARR |
| M12 | $80K MRR | $35K/mo | $1M ARR, 2,800 Pro seats |
| M18 | $140K MRR | $40K/mo | $1.7M ARR, Series A ready |

**Speaker note:**
The model is conservative — it assumes no viral coefficient and pure self-serve. Month 6 is when the Team tier launches and expansion revenue kicks in. The inflection at M12 reflects what we see in comparable PLG dev tools: 6-month lag between free signups and Pro conversion as teams validate before paying. Burn is low because this is a single engineer + infrastructure today.
